title: hieracium pilosella subsp. pervirescens
intro: Hieracium pilosella subsp. pervirescens, commonly known as a lesser plantain or false dandelion, is a plant species that belongs to the dandelion family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. The therapeutic actions of Hieracium pilosella subsp. pervirescens include its ability to stimulate digestion, improve liver function, and support the immune system. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Hieracium pilosella subsp. pervirescens can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its irritating properties. Prolonged use or excessive consumption can also lead to liver and kidney damage, as well as interact with certain medications. In rare cases, it may cause allergic reactions, including skin rashes, itching, and difficulty breathing. If ingested in large quantities or over an extended period, it may cause more severe symptoms, such as vomiting blood, stomach ulcers, and even organ failure.
